goal: Promote the `understand_code` MCP tool from a `_meta.not_yet_implemented: true` stub to a real handler that returns a structured explanation of a file or symbol by combining (a) a tree-sitter AST summary (language, LOC, import count, top-level symbol roster) and (b) knowledge-graph facts (entity metadata + relations) — satisfying feature P1-W4-F09 and master-plan §3.2 row 1 / §18 Phase 1 Week 4 line 1751.
plan_summary: Wire a new `handle_understand_code(id, params, kg)` dispatch branch in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs::McpServer::handle_tools_call`, mirroring the KG-gated pattern established by WO-0033 (find_definition), WO-0034 (get_conventions), and WO-0035 (search_code). The handler extracts `arguments.target` (required string), `arguments.kind` (optional enum `"file" | "symbol" | "module"`, default auto-detected), and `arguments.root` (optional string → PathBuf, default `std::env::current_dir`). On kind=`file` (or auto-detected when `target` resolves to an existing regular file under `root`), it reads the file contents, detects the tree-sitter language from extension via `ucil_treesitter::Language::from_extension` (add this helper if missing), parses with `ucil_treesitter::Parser::parse`, extracts symbols with `SymbolExtractor::extract`, counts lines + imports, queries `KnowledgeGraph::list_entities_by_file` for the canonicalised path, and assembles an `UnderstandCodeFileSummary` payload. On kind=`symbol`, it queries `KnowledgeGraph::get_entity_by_qualified_name`, then enumerates `list_relations_by_source` + `list_relations_by_target`, and returns an `UnderstandCodeSymbolSummary` payload. Module mode is treated as file mode for the target file. The response envelope carries `_meta: {tool: "understand_code", source: "tree-sitter+kg", kind, target, ...}` with `content[0].text` a short human-readable summary. Add frozen acceptance test `test_understand_code_tool` as a module-level `#[cfg(test)] #[test]` item in `server.rs` (alongside `test_find_definition_tool` / `test_get_conventions_tool` / `test_search_code_basic`), plus five supplementary negative/edge-case tests. All error paths use `-32602` per the JSON-RPC spec.
scope_in: New public helper `ucil_treesitter::Language::from_extension(ext: &str) -> Option<Language>` in `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/parser.rs` (IF not already present) that maps `.rs` → `Rust`, `.py` → `Python`, `.ts`/`.tsx` → `TypeScript`, `.js`/`.jsx` → `JavaScript`, `.go` → `Go`, and every other extension covered by `SUPPORTED_LANGUAGES` to its `Language` variant. Return `None` for unknown extensions. Re-export via `crates/ucil-treesitter/src/lib.rs` if not already re-exported. Rustdoc references master-plan §18 Phase 1 Week 2 line 1744 (multi-language parser) and feature `P1-W4-F09`. `#[cfg(test)]` unit tests covering at least 5 supported extensions + one unknown extension. NOTE: if this helper already exists (either inherent on `Language` or a free function in `parser.rs`), the implementation MUST NOT duplicate it — reuse the existing helper unmodified and skip this scope-in item.

New `pub(crate) struct UnderstandCodeFileSummary { pub language: &'static str, pub file_path: PathBuf, pub line_count: u64, pub import_count: usize, pub top_level_symbols: Vec<FileSymbolRow>, pub kg_entity_count: usize }` and `pub(crate) struct FileSymbolRow { pub kind: String, pub name: String, pub qualified_name: Option<String>, pub start_line: u32 }` in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` (or a new `crates/ucil-daemon/src/understand_code.rs` module — executor's call, pick whichever matches the search_code / find_definition module layout best). Derive `serde::Serialize + Debug + Clone + PartialEq`. `FileSymbolRow.qualified_name` is populated by matching the tree-sitter symbol's `name + start_line` against KG entities returned from `list_entities_by_file`; when no match exists, `qualified_name` is `None`.

New `pub(crate) struct UnderstandCodeSymbolSummary { pub entity: EntitySummary, pub inbound_relation_count: usize, pub outbound_relation_count: usize, pub inbound_edges: Vec<RelationEdge>, pub outbound_edges: Vec<RelationEdge>, pub containing_file: Option<UnderstandCodeFileSummary> }` where `EntitySummary` projects the subset of `Entity` fields safe to emit (`qualified_name`, `name`, `entity_type`, `file_path`, `start_line`, `end_line`, `signature`, `doc_comment`) and `RelationEdge { relation_type: String, peer_qualified_name: String, peer_file_path: Option<String>, peer_start_line: Option<u32> }` carries the resolved peer via `get_entity_by_id`. Cap `inbound_edges` and `outbound_edges` at `UNDERSTAND_CODE_MAX_EDGES: usize = 50` with a `tracing::warn` when truncated.

New `handle_understand_code(id: &Value, params: &Value, kg: &Arc<Mutex<KnowledgeGraph>>) -> Value` method in `server.rs` that: (1) extracts required `arguments.target: &str` — missing / non-string → `-32602` with message `understand_code: `arguments.target` is required and must be a non-empty string`; reject empty string with the same code; (2) extracts optional `arguments.kind: Option<&str>` — reject non-string as `-32602`; reject values outside the set `{"file", "symbol", "module"}` as `-32602`; default `None` triggers auto-detection; (3) extracts optional `arguments.root: Option<&str>` → `PathBuf`, default `std::env::current_dir()?`; reject non-string as `-32602`; canonicalise via `Path::canonicalize`; reject a root that does not exist or is not a directory as `-32602`; (4) auto-detect: if `kind` is unset AND `target` resolves to an existing regular file under `root` (join `root` with `target`, canonicalise, check file type) → treat as `file`; else treat as `symbol`; (5) dispatch to `explain_file` or `explain_symbol` private helpers as appropriate; (6) return the structured envelope `{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":..., "result":{"_meta":{"tool":"understand_code","source":"tree-sitter+kg","kind":..., "target":..., "summary":<payload>},"content":[{"type":"text","text":<human summary>}],"isError":false}}` on success; (7) surface file-read errors, parse errors, and KG errors as `-32603` (internal error) with a descriptive message that never leaks absolute paths outside the caller-supplied `root`.

Private helper `explain_file(target_path: &Path, root: &Path, kg: &Arc<Mutex<KnowledgeGraph>>) -> Result<UnderstandCodeFileSummary, UnderstandCodeError>` that: (a) reads the file contents via `std::fs::read_to_string`; (b) detects the language via `Language::from_extension` on the file extension; files with unknown extensions fail fast with a typed `UnsupportedLanguage` error; (c) parses with `ucil_treesitter::Parser::new().parse(&source, lang)`; (d) extracts symbols with `SymbolExtractor::extract(&tree, &source, &target_path, lang)`; (e) counts lines (`source.lines().count()`) and imports (language-aware: Rust `use`, Python `import`/`from`, TypeScript/JavaScript `import`/`require`, Go `import`; keep this as a dedicated `count_imports(source: &str, lang: Language) -> usize` free function with `#[cfg(test)]` unit tests — when in doubt, fall back to a tree-sitter query over `import` / `use` node kinds for the target language, NOT a string `.contains` scan, so comments inside strings don't inflate the count); (f) acquires the KG mutex and calls `list_entities_by_file(canonicalised_target_path_str)`; (g) builds `FileSymbolRow` entries by zipping tree-sitter symbols with KG entities on `(name, start_line)`; (h) caps `top_level_symbols` at `UNDERSTAND_CODE_MAX_TOP_LEVEL_SYMBOLS: usize = 100` with a `tracing::warn` when truncated. Returns `UnderstandCodeFileSummary`.

Private helper `explain_symbol(target: &str, kg: &Arc<Mutex<KnowledgeGraph>>, root: &Path) -> Result<UnderstandCodeSymbolSummary, UnderstandCodeError>` that: (a) acquires the KG mutex and calls `get_entity_by_qualified_name(target)`; `None` → return a typed `NotFound` error surfaced as the `not_found` shape below (the caller converts it into a well-formed `_meta.found == false` envelope per the find_definition precedent); (b) runs `list_relations_by_source(entity.id.unwrap())` and `list_relations_by_target(entity.id.unwrap())`; (c) projects each relation's peer by `get_entity_by_id(peer_id)`, dropping any relation whose peer is missing (logged at `tracing::debug`); (d) builds `RelationEdge` rows sorted by `(relation_type, peer_qualified_name)` — deterministic order is required for the acceptance test; (e) if `entity.file_path` is a real file under `root`, ALSO builds a `containing_file: Some(UnderstandCodeFileSummary)` via `explain_file` for that path; file-not-found or non-file parent is logged at `tracing::debug` and returns `containing_file: None` — never propagate as an error.

Symbol-not-found shape: when `get_entity_by_qualified_name` returns `None`, `handle_understand_code` MUST return a successful envelope with `_meta.found == false` and `isError == false` (NOT a JSON-RPC error), matching the find_definition UX contract (server.rs:589-592 rustdoc). The `_meta` block still carries `tool`, `source`, `kind`, `target`; no `summary` field is emitted in the not-found case.

New `pub(crate) enum UnderstandCodeError` in the same module that owns the handler, `#[derive(thiserror::Error, Debug)]` with variants: `Io(#[from] std::io::Error)`, `Parse(#[from] ucil_treesitter::ParseError)`, `KnowledgeGraph(#[from] KnowledgeGraphError)`, `UnsupportedLanguage(String)`, `TargetNotInRoot(String)`, `NotFound(String)`. All variants carry user-safe messages — absolute paths under `root` are stripped to relative via `strip_prefix(root).unwrap_or(path)` before embedding.

Extend `handle_tools_call` in `server.rs` with a fourth KG-gated dispatch branch exactly analogous to the existing search_code / get_conventions / find_definition branches: `if name == "understand_code" { if let Some(kg) = self.kg.as_ref() { return Self::handle_understand_code(id, params, kg); } }` positioned before the stub path. No other existing dispatch branch changes. The stub path remains untouched so phase-1 invariant #9 stays green for the remaining 18 tools (19 minus understand_code).

Acceptance test `test_understand_code_tool` as a module-level `#[cfg(test)] #[test]` item in `server.rs`, placed adjacent to `test_search_code_basic`, so the nextest selector `server::test_understand_code_tool` resolves per DEC-0007 and WO-0006 test-selector rule. Test body (file-mode happy path): (a) create `tempfile::TempDir` as the project root; write `src/lib.rs` containing a small Rust file with two imports (`use std::collections::HashMap;` and `use serde::Deserialize;`), one struct (`pub struct Config { name: String }`), and two functions (`pub fn new_config() -> Config`, `pub fn name_len(c: &Config) -> usize`); (b) open a KG via `KnowledgeGraph::open` on a second temp path; (c) upsert two `Entity` rows matching `new_config` and `name_len` with canonicalised `file_path`, `start_line` 1-based matching the source positions, `entity_type` `"function"`, and `qualified_name` `"project::lib::new_config"` / `"project::lib::name_len"`; (d) wrap KG in `Arc::new(Mutex::new(kg))`; (e) build `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph(kg)`; (f) invoke `server.handle_line` with `tools/call` request for `understand_code` with `arguments: {target: "src/lib.rs", root: <tempdir>, kind: "file"}`; (g) assert: response has no `error`; `_meta.tool == "understand_code"`; `_meta.source == "tree-sitter+kg"`; `_meta.kind == "file"`; `_meta.summary.language == "rust"`; `_meta.summary.line_count >= 6`; `_meta.summary.import_count == 2`; `_meta.summary.top_level_symbols.len() >= 3` (struct + 2 functions); at least one `top_level_symbols` row has `qualified_name == "project::lib::new_config"` (proving KG-tree-sitter zip worked); `_meta.summary.kg_entity_count == 2`; `result.isError == false`; `_meta.not_yet_implemented` is absent. Every assertion uses real tree-sitter + real SQLite — no mocks.

Supplementary `#[cfg(test)]` tests adjacent to the main acceptance test: (i) `test_understand_code_tool_symbol_mode` — same fixture; call with `arguments: {target: "project::lib::new_config", kind: "symbol"}`; assert `_meta.summary.entity.qualified_name == "project::lib::new_config"`, `_meta.summary.outbound_edges` populated with at least zero entries (sort order deterministic), `_meta.summary.containing_file.Some`; (ii) `test_understand_code_tool_auto_detect_file` — call with `arguments: {target: "src/lib.rs"}` (no explicit `kind`, file exists under cwd-substitute `root`); assert auto-detection picks file mode (`_meta.kind == "file"`); (iii) `test_understand_code_tool_missing_target` — omit `arguments.target`; assert `error.code == -32602`; (iv) `test_understand_code_tool_empty_target` — `arguments.target: ""`; assert `error.code == -32602`; (v) `test_understand_code_tool_unknown_symbol` — call with `arguments: {target: "project::does_not_exist", kind: "symbol"}`; assert `isError == false`, `_meta.found == false`, `_meta.summary` absent; (vi) `test_understand_code_tool_no_kg_returns_stub` — `McpServer::new()` (no KG attached); assert response carries `_meta.not_yet_implemented == true` (phase-1 invariant #9); (vii) `test_understand_code_tool_invalid_kind` — `arguments.kind: "galaxy"`; assert `error.code == -32602`.

Rustdoc on every new public / `pub(crate)` item (`Language::from_extension` if added, `UnderstandCodeFileSummary`, `FileSymbolRow`, `UnderstandCodeSymbolSummary`, `EntitySummary`, `RelationEdge`, `UnderstandCodeError`, `handle_understand_code`, `explain_file`, `explain_symbol`, `count_imports`, `UNDERSTAND_CODE_MAX_EDGES`, `UNDERSTAND_CODE_MAX_TOP_LEVEL_SYMBOLS`) referencing: master-plan §3.2 row 1 (`understand_code` — explain what a file/function/module does), master-plan §18 Phase 1 Week 4 line 1751 (Phase 1 deliverable), master-plan §12.1 (entities schema), master-plan §12.2 (relations schema), phase-1 CLAUDE.md invariant #9 (other 18 tools keep the stub after this WO), feature-list:P1-W4-F09 frozen selector `-p ucil-daemon server::test_understand_code_tool`, and DEC-0007 (mutation verification via stash-based reality-check).

`ready-for-review.md` at `ucil-build/work-orders/0036-ready-for-review.md` summarising the diff, commits, acceptance outcome, and a stash-based reality-check evidence block per the WO-0033 / WO-0034 / WO-0035 precedent.
scope_out: Semantic / vector explanation via LanceDB embeddings (master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 8). This WO delivers ONLY the structural (G1) + pattern-roster summary; semantic-first 'understand_code' enrichment is explicitly Phase 2.

Cross-module / cross-file resolution when kind=`module`. This WO maps `module` kind to `file` mode for the single target file; real module walking (manifest parse, dependency graph) arrives in Phase 2 via `trace_dependencies` infrastructure.

CEQP universal-parameter semantic processing (`reason`, `task_goal`, `files_in_context`, `non_obvious_info_agent_has`). The schema advertises them (P1-W4-F06 passing); this WO reads them from `arguments` but does NOT act on them. Applying `reason` for enrichment is a Phase-2+ concern.
Serena / LSP enrichment (hover info, type chains). This WO uses only tree-sitter + KG. Serena fusion is P1-W5-F02 and is a separate WO.

Full G3 (patterns/conventions) fusion beyond what already lives in the KG via P1-W4-F02 + P1-W4-F04 + WO-0034. This WO does NOT re-run convention inference — it reads from `conventions` only if a future enrichment step calls it (Phase 2 scope).
File-watcher-driven re-parse on edit. The handler reads the file at call time; incremental re-parse is P1-W3-F02's job (passing).

Handler wiring for the remaining 18 MCP tools still stubbed after WO-0033/0034/0035/0036 (`find_similar`, `get_context_for_edit`, `get_architecture`, `trace_dependencies`, `blast_radius`, `explain_history`, `remember`, `review_changes`, `check_quality`, `run_tests`, `security_scan`, `lint_code`, `type_check`, `refactor`, `generate_docs`, `query_database`, `check_runtime`, `find_references`).
Changes to any file under `tests/fixtures/**`, `scripts/gate/**`, `scripts/flip-feature.sh`, `ucil-build/feature-list.json`, `ucil-build/feature-list.schema.json`, or the master plan.
Changes to the 22-tool catalog or its `ucil_tools()` builder — the catalog is frozen under WO-0010's acceptance test (`server::test_all_22_tools_registered` must stay green).
Changes to `McpServer::serve` control flow beyond the single new dispatch branch in `handle_tools_call`.
Changes to the existing `find_definition` / `get_conventions` / `search_code` handlers, their helpers, or their tests.
acceptance: `understand_code` MCP tool returns a structured summary (file mode or symbol mode) driven by real tree-sitter parsing + real `KnowledgeGraph::open` + `upsert_entity` + `list_entities_by_file` / `get_entity_by_qualified_name` sequences on a real `tempfile::TempDir` with real file contents. File mode correctly zips tree-sitter top-level symbols with KG entities on `(name, start_line)`; symbol mode correctly enumerates relations with `get_entity_by_id` peer resolution.

Auto-detection: when `arguments.kind` is omitted AND `target` resolves to an existing regular file under `arguments.root` (default cwd), the handler picks file mode; otherwise symbol mode. Proven by `test_understand_code_tool_auto_detect_file`.

Symbol-not-found returns a successful envelope with `_meta.found == false`, `_meta.kind == "symbol"`, `_meta.target == <input>`, `_meta.summary` ABSENT, `isError == false` — NOT a JSON-RPC error envelope.
No KG attached: the dispatch falls through to the stub and response carries `_meta.not_yet_implemented == true` — phase-1 invariant #9 preserved.

Error paths use JSON-RPC `-32602` for: missing `arguments.target`, empty string `arguments.target`, non-string `arguments.target`, non-string `arguments.kind`, `arguments.kind` outside `{"file", "symbol", "module"}`, non-string `arguments.root`, `arguments.root` that does not exist, `arguments.root` that is not a directory.
Frozen acceptance selector `server::test_understand_code_tool` resolves under `cargo nextest` with the mainline executor profile per DEC-0007 and the WO-0006 test-selector rule.
22-tool catalog stays wire-compatible: `server::test_all_22_tools_registered` stays green and every still-stubbed tool keeps returning `_meta.not_yet_implemented: true`.

Regression safety on prior handlers: `server::test_find_definition_tool`, `server::test_get_conventions_tool`, and `server::test_search_code_basic` stay green — the new dispatch branch does not shadow or alter the existing gating.
No regressions across the workspace: `cargo nextest run --workspace` and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` stay green. `cargo fmt --all --check` is clean.

Zero new stubs introduced: `grep -nE 'todo!\\(|unimplemented!\\(|NotImplementedError|raise NotImplementedError'` across the WO diff yields zero hits. Phase-1 invariant #9 preserved for the 18 tools that still stub after this WO.
Every public / `pub(crate)` item added has rustdoc; `cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps` and `cargo doc -p ucil-treesitter --no-deps` emit zero warnings/errors.

Commit cadence per CLAUDE.md ≤50-LOC-per-commit target: (1) `Language::from_extension` helper in `ucil-treesitter` (if needed) + its unit tests; (2) `UnderstandCodeError` enum + `UnderstandCodeFileSummary` / `FileSymbolRow` / `UnderstandCodeSymbolSummary` / `EntitySummary` / `RelationEdge` structs + rustdoc; (3) `count_imports` free function + its unit tests; (4) `explain_file` helper + rustdoc; (5) `explain_symbol` helper + rustdoc; (6) `handle_understand_code` method + dispatch wiring; (7) error paths (empty target, non-string target, invalid kind, missing target, nonexistent root); (8) `test_understand_code_tool` acceptance + at least six negative tests; (9) rustdoc polish + `ready-for-review.md`. 9-11 commits total. Each commit pushed immediately per CLAUDE.md.

`reality-check.sh` (verifier step 6b) passes: stashing the `handle_understand_code` method + dispatch wiring causes `test_understand_code_tool` to FAIL (stub returns `_meta.not_yet_implemented: true`, happy-path assertions all break); popping restores GREEN. Proves the test exercises real code, not a mock.
`ready-for-review.md` exists at `ucil-build/work-orders/0036-ready-for-review.md` and summarises the implementation + acceptance outcome.
